skill.name: startup-financial-modeling
skill.description: Build 3-5 year startup financial models with revenue projections, cost structures, cash flow, runway, scenarios, and fundraising assumptions.
skill.summary: Create practical startup financial projections for planning, fundraising, and scenario analysis.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown formulas, financial notation, and plain business terms as command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance. Manual review found no executable code, no network behavior, no secret access, and no prompt injection attempt in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Formulas Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are fenced Markdown examples and inline financial formulas such as MRR, ARR, runway, cash flow, valuation, and model references. They do not invoke a shell, Ruby backticks, subprocesses, or user-controlled command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are Markdown code fences, formulas, or reference filenames. No command interpreter, executable script, or dynamic input path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Business Text Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain the skill description, financial modeling overview, design costs, and contraction MRR terminology. No weak hash algorithm, cryptographic API, key handling, or encryption logic appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines are natural-language financial planning content. There is no cryptographic operation or security-sensitive implementation to evaluate.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Expense Terms Misclassified as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged lines discuss operating expenses and cash collection timing. They do not run discovery commands, inspect the host system, read local files, or collect environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both lines are ordinary financial model text about expenses. No system command, host identifier, or reconnaissance workflow is present.

content.value_statement: Startup teams need clear projections before they can plan hiring, fundraising, and cash use. This skill structures revenue, costs, runway, and scenarios into practical financial planning outputs.
actual_capabilities: Builds 3-5 year financial projection structures for startups.
Models revenue using cohorts, retention, ARPU, pricing, and expansion assumptions.
Organizes operating expenses across COGS, sales, marketing, research, development, and administration.
Calculates burn rate, cash flow, runway, funding needs, and dilution scenarios.
Creates conservative, base, and optimistic scenarios for planning discussions.
Identifies key startup metrics such as CAC, LTV, gross margin, and burn multiple.
limitations: It provides planning guidance, not certified financial, tax, or investment advice.
Outputs depend on the quality of the assumptions supplied by the user.
It does not include executable spreadsheet files or automated accounting integrations.
Benchmark ranges are general and should be validated against current market data.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Seed Fundraising Model
use_cases.description: Create a three-year model with revenue, hiring, burn, runway, and funding needs for investor conversations.

use_cases.title: Review Operating Plan Assumptions
use_cases.description: Test whether hiring, customer acquisition, gross margin, and cash timing support the planned growth path.

use_cases.title: Compare Business Model Scenarios
use_cases.description: Model SaaS, marketplace, or e-commerce assumptions across conservative, base, and optimistic cases.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Runway Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a simple 24-month startup runway model using my current cash, monthly revenue, monthly expenses, and planned hires.

prompt_templates.title: Build a SaaS Projection
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 3-year SaaS financial model with cohorts, ARPU, churn, expansion revenue, COGS, headcount, burn, and runway.

prompt_templates.title: Stress Test Fundraising Needs
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze conservative, base, and optimistic scenarios for our next raise. Include runway, milestone timing, funding need, and dilution.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Model Assumptions
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these startup financial assumptions for risks, unrealistic growth, missing costs, cash timing issues, and investor readiness.

output_examples.input: We have $900,000 cash, $40,000 monthly revenue, $120,000 monthly expenses, and five planned hires.
output_examples.output: A runway summary showing current burn, projected hire impact, monthly cash balance, funding gap, and key assumptions to validate.

output_examples.input: Create a SaaS model for 100 starting customers, $250 ARPU, 4% monthly churn, and 15 new customers per month.
output_examples.output: A cohort-based revenue forecast with MRR, ARR, churned revenue, customer count, gross margin, and scenario notes.

output_examples.input: We may raise $4 million at a $16 million pre-money valuation. Show the planning impact.
output_examples.output: A funding scenario with post-money valuation, estimated dilution, runway extension, use of funds, and milestone coverage.

best_practices: Separate assumptions from outputs so each driver is easy to inspect.
Use three scenarios and explain the operating decision tied to each one.
Validate growth, margin, CAC, and hiring assumptions against comparable companies.
anti_patterns: Do not use a single aggressive revenue case as the operating plan.
Do not ignore cash timing, payment terms, taxes, benefits, or software costs.
Do not treat benchmark ranges as proof that a business model will work.

faq.question: What type of startup is this skill best for?
faq.answer: It is most useful for early-stage SaaS, marketplace, e-commerce, and subscription businesses that need structured projections.

faq.question: Can it create a spreadsheet file?
faq.answer: The skill describes model structure and calculations. It does not include a spreadsheet generator or file automation.

faq.question: Does it replace a finance professional?
faq.answer: No. It supports planning and analysis, but legal, tax, accounting, and investment decisions need qualified review.

faq.question: What inputs should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide cash balance, revenue drivers, pricing, churn, expenses, hiring plans, fundraising assumptions, and target milestones.

faq.question: Can it model fundraising dilution?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline pre-money valuation, investment amount, post-money valuation, dilution, and use of funds.

faq.question: Can it review an existing model?
faq.answer: Yes. It can identify risky assumptions, missing costs, weak unit economics, cash timing issues, and scenario gaps.
